content: An undamped spring-mass system with mass m and spring stiffness k is shown in the figure. The natural frequency and natural period of this system are $$\omega$$ rad/s and T s, respectively. If the stiffness of the spring is doubled and the mass is halved, then the natural frequency and the natural period of the modified system, respectively, are
explanation:
Natural frequency, $\omega_n$, is given by :

$ \omega_n = \sqrt{\frac{k}{m}} \, \text{rad/s} $

Time period, $ T_n $, is given by :

$ T_n = \frac{2\pi}{\omega_n} $

For the new system, the mass is halved ($ m' = \frac{m}{2} $) and the stiffness is doubled ($ k' = 2k $).

Therefore, the new natural frequency, $\omega'_n$, is :

$ \omega'_n = \sqrt{\frac{k'}{m'}} = \sqrt{\frac{2k}{\frac{m}{2}}} = \sqrt{\frac{4k}{m}} $

Simplifying further :

$ \omega'_n = 2\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}} $

Since $\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}} = \omega_n$ :

$ \omega'_n = 2\omega_n $

This means the frequency will be doubled.

The new time period, $ T'_n $, is given by :

$ T'_n = \frac{2\pi}{\omega'_n} = \frac{2\pi}{2\omega_n} $

Simplifying further :

$ \frac{2\pi}{\omega_n} = T_n $

$ T'_n = \frac{T_n}{2} $

Therefore, the time period will be halved.

content:
A particle of mass 2 kg is traveling at a velocity of 1.5 m/s. A force f(t)=3t<sup>2</sup> (in N) is applied
to it in the direction of motion for a duration of 2 seconds, where t denotes time in seconds.
The velocity (in m/s, up to one decimal place) of the particle immediately after the removal of
the force is________
